skill.name: firestore-security-rules-auditor
skill.description: A skill to evaluate how secure Firestore security rules are. Use this when Firestore security rules are updated to ensure that the generated rules are extremely secure and robust.
skill.summary: An AI-powered security auditor that evaluates Firebase Firestore security rules against a rigorous penetration testing checklist.
skill.author: firebase

security_audit.summary: All 8 static findings evaluated as false positives. The skill is a legitimate Firebase security auditing tool that uses security testing terminology (e.g., 'find the hole in the wall', 'red team', 'penetration testing'). No external commands, cryptographic operations, or system reconnaissance code exists. The file contains only markdown documentation for an AI auditor skill.

dangerous_patterns.title: Security Terminology False Positive
dangerous_patterns.description: Static analyzer flagged security audit terminology as dangerous patterns. Phrases like 'find the hole in the wall' (line 11), 'update bypass' (line 14), and 'type safety' (line 19) are standard penetration testing concepts, not actual vulnerabilities.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: Visual inspection confirms no actual code execution, cryptographic operations, or reconnaissance tools. Lines 11-19 contain only markdown documentation describing security audit concepts.

dangerous_patterns.title: Non-existent Line Reference
dangerous_patterns.description: Static analyzer reported 'Weak cryptographic algorithm' at SKILL.md:41, but the file has only 45 lines. This is an artifact error, not a real finding.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: File verification confirms 45 total lines - line 41 cannot exist.

content.value_statement: Manually reviewing Firestore security rules is time-consuming and error-prone. This skill automates security audits using a comprehensive penetration testing checklist to identify vulnerabilities in your Firebase rules.
actual_capabilities: Evaluate Firestore security rules against a 6-point penetration testing checklist
Detect update bypass vulnerabilities where users can modify documents into invalid states
Identify authority source issues where security relies on user-controlled data
Check for resource exhaustion risks like missing string length or array size limits
Verify type safety checks using is string, is int, and is timestamp validations
Score rules from 1 (critical) to 5 (secure) with detailed findings and recommendations
limitations: Cannot access live Firebase projects to test rules in real-time
Only evaluates rules passed directly in the prompt, not deployed rules
Cannot detect business logic flaws that require understanding your specific app architecture
Does not integrate with Firebase CLI for automatic rules deployment

use_cases.title: Review new security rules before deployment
use_cases.description: Before shipping updated Firestore rules, paste them into Claude and ask for a security audit. Get a score and actionable recommendations in seconds.

use_cases.title: Learn security best practices
use_cases.description: Study the audit checklist and findings to understand common Firestore security mistakes and how to fix them. Great for developers new to Firebase security.

use_cases.title: Security code review assistance
use_cases.description: Use this skill as a starting point for formal security code reviews. The AI findings help prioritize which vulnerabilities to address first.

prompt_templates.title: Basic security audit
prompt_templates.prompt:
I need you to audit my Firestore security rules. Here are the rules:

[paste your firestore.rules]

Please evaluate them using your security audit checklist and provide a score and findings.

prompt_templates.title: Detailed review with business context
prompt_templates.prompt:
Review these Firestore security rules for my collaboration app. The rules should allow collaborators to read shared documents but only owners can delete them. Here are my rules:

[paste rules]

Does my implementation match these requirements?

prompt_templates.title: Compare create vs update rules
prompt_templates.prompt:
Check if my Firestore rules have an update bypass vulnerability. Specifically, can a user create a document and then update the 'role' or 'isAdmin' field to gain unauthorized privileges? Here are the rules:

[paste rules]

Analyze the 'create' and 'update' rules carefully.

prompt_templates.title: Production readiness check
prompt_templates.prompt: Perform a full security audit of these Firestore rules before I deploy to production. Check all six items in your audit checklist and give me a score from 1-5.

output_examples.output:
Score: 4 (Minor)

Findings:
- check: 'Resource Exhaustion'
  severity: minor
  issue: 'No string length limits on user data fields'
  recommendation: 'Add max() constraints for text fields'
- check: 'Type Safety'
  severity: minor
  issue: 'No type validation for fields like displayName'
  recommendation: 'Use is string, is int checks'

Score: 2 (Major)

Findings:
- check: 'Authority Source'
  severity: major
  issue: 'Any authenticated user can write to any post'
  recommendation: 'Add ownership check: resource.data.authorId == request.auth.uid'
- check: 'Business Logic'
  severity: major
  issue: 'No restrictions prevent users from deleting others posts'
  recommendation: 'Require ownership for delete operations'

best_practices: Always add ownership checks for document writes: resource.data.uid == request.auth.uid
Use type validation (is string, is int) for all user-provided fields to prevent data corruption
Set size limits on string and array fields to prevent resource exhaustion attacks
anti_patterns: Do not rely on request.resource.data for sensitive fields like 'role' or 'isAdmin'
Do not use request.auth != null alone without additional authorization checks
Do not leave 'allow read: if true' on documents that contain user-specific data

faq.question: How accurate is the security audit score?
faq.answer: The AI follows a rigorous 6-point penetration testing checklist. Results are deterministic based on the checklist criteria. However, it cannot detect business logic flaws specific to your app.

faq.question: Can this replace a manual security review?
faq.answer: This skill is a useful starting point but should not replace manual review for production systems. Use it to catch common mistakes and prioritize your security review effort.

faq.question: What does a score of 5 mean?
faq.answer: A score of 5 indicates comprehensive validation, strict ownership enforcement, and proper role-based access control. Your rules follow Firebase security best practices.

faq.question: How do I interpret findings marked as 'critical'?
faq.answer: Critical findings indicate unauthorized data access, privilege escalation, or validation bypass. Fix these immediately before deploying your rules.

faq.question: Does this work with Realtime Database rules?
faq.answer: No, this skill is designed specifically for Firestore security rules. The syntax and capabilities differ from Realtime Database rules.

faq.question: Can I use this to audit rules on my Firebase project?
faq.answer: Copy your rules from the Firebase console or your rules file and paste them into Claude. The skill evaluates the rules text you provide, not live project data.
